title: Los Angeles, CA – Pedestrian Killed in Train Accident near Media Center Drive in Glassell ParkAccording to the Los Angeles Fire Department, the incident was reported at around 11:30 a.m. March 11, in the area of 2705 Media Center Drive west of San Ferna - facebook.com
content:
Los Angeles, CA – Pedestrian Killed in Train Accident near Media Center Drive in Glassell ParkAccording to the Los Angeles Fire Department, the incident was reported at around 11:30 a.m. March 11, in the area of 2705 Media Center Drive west of San Fernando Road

Los Angeles, CA – Authorities responded to an accident in which a pedestrian was hit and killed by a train in the Glassell Park neighborhood of Los Angeles on Wednesday morning.

According to the Los Angeles Fire Department, the incident was reported at around 11:30 a.m. March 11, in the area of 2705 Media Center Drive west of San Fernando Road.

Initial reports indicate that an Amtrak train hit a person on the tracks. It’s unclear why the victim was on the tracks when they were hit.

The Los Angeles Fire Department crews and Los Angeles Police Department officers arrived at the scene shortly after.

The medics confirmed the pedestrian’s death at the site of the collision. The victim’s name, age, and gender were not immediately released.

No one on the train reported any injuries as a result of the collision.

At this time, no additional details on the incident have been released. Investigations are ongoing to establish how and why the accident occurred.

https://www.lapdonline.org/ According to 2025 year-end data from the Los Angeles Police Department (), the city recorded 290 traffic fatalities, a slight 6% decrease from the 302 deaths reported in 2024. Despite this modest decline, the roads remain deadlier than pre-pandemic levels. Pedestrians remain the most vulnerable group, accounting for over 50% of all fatalities (150+ deaths in 2025), while severe injuries citywide have remained high, consistently exceeding 1,500 annually since 2022.

title: Yexenia Gonzalez killed in Glassell Park train crash, LAPD says - The Legal Advocate
content:
Struck by Amtrak train between Glendale and L.A. Union stations
LOS ANGELES, CA, (March 11, 2026) - A 46-year-old woman was tragically killed in a pedestrian-train accident Wednesday in Glassell Park, a neighborhood in northeast Los Angeles.
Yexenia Gonzalez died after being struck by an Amtrak train on Media Center Drive between the Glendale and L.A. Union stations.
According to the Los Angeles Police Department, Yexenia Gonzalez was struck by Amtrak Pacific Surfliner train 774 between the Glendale and L.A. Union stations. It happened at about 11:30 a.m. in the area of 2705 Media Drive, east of North San Fernando Road.
Gonzalez was pronounced deceased at the scene.
FATAL TRAIN ACCIDENT INVESTIGATION
It was unclear how the woman ended up in the path of the train. Her manner of death wasn't disclosed by the medical examiner’s office.
Train accidents such as this can occur for several reasons, including train personnel error, train track failure, a mechanical error or malfunction, a signalman error, and reckless actions.
According to the National Transportation Safety Bureau (NTSB), approximately 1,000 people are killed in train-related accidents each year. During the probe, investigators will also examine if the crash site was made safe for the pedestrian crossing.
Candice Bond, an experienced train accident attorney, said that in cases such as this that it is imperative to have an independent investigation conducted by a seasoned lawyer as soon as possible to preserve evidence for a victim's family.
“A skilled attorney can protect a grieving family from powerful train companies, which often send their investigators to crash sites and possess a variety of resources they can utilize to defend a negligent party,” said Bond, Managing Partner of Bond Legal and national analyst for The Legal Advocate.
WRONGFUL DEATH CLAIM
Pending results of the investigation, the family members of Yexenia Gonzalez may be entitled to a wrongful death claim. Family members of wrongful death victims may seek damages for loss of love and companionship, pain, suffering and emotional distress following a catastrophic accident such as this incident.
“The stress and sorrow can be extremely overwhelming after losing a loved one in an accident,” said Stefano Formica, Of Counsel with Bond Legal. “We understand just what families are forced to go through, and having their rights protected is vital. A wrongful death lawyer will stay updated on the official investigation and help ensure that their legal rights and best interests are protected.”

title: Deadly Train Accident Near Glassell Park Warehouse - Hoodline
content:
A pedestrian was struck and killed by a train late Wednesday morning in Glassell Park, in a deadly collision that unfolded near a warehouse-lined stretch of tracks. The crash was reported around 11:30 a.m. near 2705 Media Center Drive, where emergency crews pronounced the victim dead at the scene. Responders reported no other patients connected to the incident.

Scene and response

According to the Los Angeles Fire Department, crews from Fire Station 50, along with multiple battalion, engine, and rescue units, responded to the rail emergency and logged the call as Inc#0734. Fire officials reported no additional patients on or around the train and said the Los Angeles Police Department and the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner would handle body recovery under LAPD Inc#2132.

Where it happened

The crash took place in the Media Center industrial corridor of northeast Los Angeles, near a warehouse property at 2705 Media Center Drive. Public records list the address as a large one-story warehouse and distribution site, and the location underscores how rail right-of-way cuts through mixed industrial and residential stretches in the neighborhood. PropertyShark confirms the site details.

Investigation and context

Pedestrian safety continues to pose problems across Los Angeles, and the LAPD has highlighted targeted enforcement and public outreach as part of ongoing efforts to curb traffic deaths. Train-involved collisions in the region have also led to lengthy investigations and service disruptions in the past, as reported by the Los Angeles Times, a reminder of how a single trackside incident can ripple through the broader transportation system.

What's next

LAPD traffic investigators are handling the on-scene probe, while the County medical examiner oversees body recovery and will determine the cause and manner of death. Authorities have not released any identifying information about the victim, and the investigation remains active as agencies say they will share additional details when they are available.

title: Family of Atwater man found dead near railroad tracks raising money for funeral - Merced Sun-Star
content:
The mother of an Atwater man found dead near a railroad crossing on Sunday night has started a GoFundMe fundraiser to help pay for his funeral expenses.

The family is looking to raise money to give Lucas Alexander Raphel Benavidez, 26, a proper funeral.

As of the morning of Friday, March 13, $1,506 raised of the goal of $2K has been raised.

Atwater police closed the railroad crossings on Atwater Boulevard at Applegate Road and on Packers and High Street at about 6 p.m. Sunday to investigate, the Atwater Police Department said on Facebook. The crossings were open a few hours later.

“He was taken from us far too soon in a tragic accident involving a train, leaving a void in our family, our hearts, our lives and those of countless others that can never be filled,” Heather Burns said in the GoFundMe post.

This story was originally published March 11, 2026 at 8:12 AM.

title: Train kills person in San Mateo - Palo Alto Daily Post
content:
Caltrain photo.

A Caltrain hit and killed a person on Saturday in San Mateo, authorities confirmed today (March 16).
The person who died has not been publicly identified by the San Mateo County Coroner’s Office, pending notification of their next of kin.
The crash occurred near E. Bellevue Ave. around 5:30 p.m.
Caltrain has a policy of no longer telling the public about deaths on the tracks in an attempt to prevent copycat suicides. It is not known whether Saturday’s death was an accident or suicide.
Caltrain reported 10 deaths in 2022, 15 deaths in 2023 and 19 deaths in 2024 — the most in a decade.
Another 10 people died last year, according to a fatality log provided by the agency.
If you or someone you know is having a mental health crisis, help is available. Call or text the 24/7 suicide and crisis lifeline at 988.

Woman struck by Amtrak Pacific Surfliner train east of North San Fernando Road
March 11, 2026 | Los Angeles, CA
A tragic incident unfolded in the Glassell Park area of northeast Los Angeles when a pedestrian lost her life after colliding with a moving train.
Authorities responded swiftly to the scene, where emergency efforts proved unable to save the victim.
Yexenia Gonzalez, 46, died after an Amtrak Pacific Surfliner train struck a pedestrian near 2705 Media Drive in the Glassell Park area of Los Angeles.
What Happened in the Crash?
The Los Angeles Police Department reported that Yexenia Gonzalez, aged 46, was hit by Amtrak Pacific Surfliner train 774. The collision occurred around 11:30 a.m. near 2705 Media Drive, just east of North San Fernando Road, between the Glendale and Los Angeles Union stations.

- Outcome: Victim pronounced dead at the scene

Why Do Train Accidents Like This Occur?
Details surrounding how Gonzalez came into contact with the train remain under review. The Los Angeles County Medical Examiner's Office has not yet released information on the exact manner of death.
Common factors in pedestrian-train incidents include errors by train operators, issues with track infrastructure, mechanical failures in the locomotive, signaling mistakes, or unsafe pedestrian behavior. Officials are assessing whether the area provided adequate safety measures for crossings.
According to the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB), roughly 1,000 individuals lose their lives annually in accidents involving trains across the United States. This statistic underscores the persistent risks associated with rail travel and pedestrian interactions.

Robert Rufus Thomas Jr., 49, was fatally hit by an Amtrak train near the Salinas station Monday night.
